creator: gslin@gslin.org
text:
On Mon, Aug 11, 2008 at 07:51:51PM +0200, Lupe Christoph wrote:
> On Tuesday, 2008-08-12 at 01:26:34 +0800, Gea-Suan Lin wrote:
> > On Mon, Aug 11, 2008 at 09:38:58AM +0200, Lupe Christoph wrote:
> > > But both ports will accept a pre-existing UID and GID. So you just have
> > > to make your own "munin" user and group and it will happily accept
> > > those.
> > Thanks! I didn't get this idea before.  I'll try to use this method to
> > deploy it.
> Before you do (if you haven't already), check if these commands show you
> the existing user and group:
> pw user show munin
> pw group show munin
> 
> If they don't, we need something more powerful ...
> 
> Lupe Christoph

Those command run quite well.

gslin@aqua [~] [2:00/W5] pw user show munin
munin:*:494:494::0:0::/:/usr/sbin/nologin
gslin@aqua [~] [2:00/W5] pw group show munin
munin:*:494:

-- 
* Gea-Suan Lin                                    http://blog.gslin.org/
* If you cannot convince them, confuse them.           -- Harry S Truman
